scope: FQ-09M: readable action feedback (mining swing, placement, casting, hurt/collapse, enemy hits, craft/forge confirmations) with zero changes to gameplay timing, drops, damage numbers, recipes, saves, or input bindings; plus intake of the Codex lane's FQ-09U adaptive-score assets with runtime playback deliberately not wired.
summary: FQ-09M shipped as one small reusable primitive plus per-action wiring. scripts/fx/action_fx.gd is the repo's first transient-effect pattern: five deterministic kinds (place_pulse expanding box, hit_spark radial burst, cast_ring plotted circle, dust_puff rising fan, forge_spark confirmation) drawn at stepped 10 Hz with no randomness, all under 0.4 s, self-freeing, in the action_fx test group, spawned through a null-safe static helper. The mining tool swing lives in player._draw: an arm and a pick-or-axe glyph (axe when the target is axe-preferred and an axe is carried) cycling raise/mid/strike six pose-steps per second toward the target side, derived purely from live mining state via the swing_phase() hook - it can never write back into mining timing, and the dirt-21/trunk-33/stone-66/axe-24 frame baselines pass unchanged. Confirmations route through one game_root._craft_confirm_fx choke point (hall forges burst at the hall, hand crafting at the player). Seven fq09m_* checks (suite 217/217) prove the hooks, the exact one-effect-per-event counts, and that the effect group empties within lifetimes - transience by construction. In the same run, a separate commit took in the Codex lane's rendered adaptive suite (4 contexts, 6 stems, 5 stingers, m8patch, render/verify tooling; loops exactly 16 bars at 72 BPM/48 kHz, stingers under 8 s, all 63 stem mixes below full scale). Nothing at runtime reads audio/music yet; FQ-09U1's remaining gates are the Godot 4.6 audio spike and the operator's listening approval, both recorded as pending. Operator-side .rar archives were ignore-listed rather than committed.
acceptance: Every FQ-09M action has visible feedback: swing arc + strike rhythm (mining/chopping), placement pulse, cast ring, hurt spark + collapse/respawn dust, enemy hit spark + death dust, craft/forge confirmation burst
All pre-existing timing/drop/damage/save checks remain green at 217/217; the fq09m section runs before them and restores nothing because it changes nothing persistent
Animation state is transient by construction: fq09m_fx_transient proves the action_fx group empties; the swing derives from live mining state; no save schema touched
Asset intake preserved every boundary: runtime isolation verified by grep, listening gate recorded as pending, packaging archives excluded
Haiku verifier NO FINDINGS across both change sets
validation: COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 217/217 PASS at 2026-07-10T17:04:51
scripts/audio/verify_music_assets.py (Codex lane) -> PASS; operator listening approval PENDING
decisions: One tiny effect node instead of an animation framework: five kinds cover every FQ-09M surface; deterministic stepped 10 Hz timing matches the house style established by the opening cinematic
One _craft_confirm_fx choke point instead of five per-site effects - forges and hand crafting stay visually consistent and future stations inherit it
The swing is state-derived presentation (swing_phase from mine_progress), so it is provably incapable of altering mining timing
The Codex asset drop was committed separately from FQ-09M with its pending listening gate stated in the commit message - evidence over blending
Operator-side .rar packaging archives are ignore-listed (audio/**/*.rar) - only OGGs, sources, and tooling belong in the public repo

Concurrency incident recorded: the Codex lane hardened two in-flight fq09m smoke checks while dropping assets; changes were reviewed and kept, and the lanes-do-not-share-files rule is restated for future runs
Swarm: 2 haiku seam scouts, lead implementation, 1 haiku diff verifier
risks: Effect feel (sizes, lifetimes, colors) untested by human play; constants live in action_fx.gd
Swing anchor points are tuned to the placeholder rect body and must move when ancestry sprites land
FQ-09U1 must wait for the Godot spike evidence and the operator's listening approval
The rendered suite's musical quality has passed only mechanical checks - the human gate is the real one
next_action: FQ-09U1 once gated items clear: operator listens to the four context loops and stems; Codex delivers the Godot 4.6 audio spike evidence.
